title: Lost Reef
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image= |name=''Lost Reef'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]]''[[Stock Ships]]'' |line=[[DeepWater-class light freighter|''DeepWater''-class light freighter]] |model= |type=[[Light freighter/Legends|Light freighter]] |cost=76,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |modifier=*[[Saell-Terae]] *[[Jorj Car'das]]''[[Choices of One]]'' |sysmods= |length=45 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=1,050 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]]/[[Standard hour/Legends|h]] |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive=*Class 1.0 *Backup class 10 |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=*Military grade [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] *Can be modified for [[Water/Legends|underwater]] use *Backup [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|generator]] |hull= |sensor= |target=Improved [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]] |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*Quad [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] turret (1) *Concealed [[Missile launcher/Legends|torpedo launchers]] (4) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=2 |skeleton=1 |passengers=6 |capacity=85 [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] |cargohandling= |consumables=2 [[Standard month/Legends|months]] |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=[[Battle of the Poln system]] |affiliation=*[[Mon Calamari Commercial Expeditionary Service]] *[[Car'das/Legends|Car'das]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=*Saell-Terae *Jorj Car'das |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|The Mon Cals build their ships to last, and I put in a few extra modifications after you gave her to me. She'll hold together.|Jorj Car'das to Thrawn|}} The '''''Lost Reef''''' was a [[DeepWater-class light freighter|''DeepWater''-class light freighter]] produced by [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]] for the use of [[Mon Calamari Commercial Expeditionary Service]]. During its rule, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] dissolved that organization, and the ''Lost Reef'' was sold in auction. It was purchased by a [[Yarkora/Legends|Yarkora]] antiquities dealer, [[Saell-Terae]], who used the ship to map the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. After an extended stay in the Unknown Regions Saell-Terae abandoned the ''Lost Reef'' and the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was discovered empty and adrift near the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]], carrying [[Three warrior statues|three valuable statues]] in a [[Smuggling compartment/Legends|hidden compartment]] as well as a [[Dataplaque/Legends|dataplaque]] with [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]] [[Astrogation map|charts]] for a part of the Unknown Regions. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], the ''Lost Reef'' was coveted by its owner Saell-Terae, as well as by the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]—he was interested in the three statues that the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] carried, as their design would provide valuable information on his enemies. At some point before [[0 ABY/Legends|0.8 ABY]], the ''Lost Reef'' had fallen into Thrawn's hands. In turn, he presented the ship as a gift to his longtime friend, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jorj Car'das]]. When the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Nuso Esva]] threatened the [[Poln system/Legends|Poln system]]—located at the border between the Unknown Regions and the Empire—Thrawn enlisted Car'das' help. The ''Lost Reef'' was essential in turning the tide of the [[battle of the Poln system]], when Thrawn used it to prevent a collision between the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] and the [[Golan I Space Defense Platform/Legends|Golan station]] guarding [[Poln Major]]. ==Description== {{Quote|You really think this is going to work?''"
"''The theory is perfectly sound. The only question is whether or not the ''Lost Reef'' will be able to handle the strain.''"
"''Don't worry about that. The Mon Cals build their ships to last, and I put in a lot of modifications after you gave her to me. She'll hold together.|Jorj Car'das and Thrawn, during the battle of the Poln system|}} [[File:DeepWater.png|thumb|left|180px|A basic model of a ''DeepWater''-class freighter]] The ''Lost Reef'' was one of the 10,000 [[DeepWater-class light freighter|''DeepWater''-class light freighters]] that [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]] built for use by the [[Mon Calamari Commercial Expeditionary Service]] before the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] dissolved the organization. As was typical with [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] designs, the ''Lost Reef'' had unique features, which differentiated it in small, mostly cosmetic ways from every other ship in its class. However, it shared one unusual feature with other ships of the same type: it could be used both in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and [[Water/Legends|underwater]] with relatively small changes required for the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. Other features that the ''Lost Reef'' shared with its sister ships included a modest cargo room—it could only carry eighty-five [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] in its two holds—length of forty-five [[Meter/Legends|meters]], and a reliable set of [[Engine/Legends|engines]], which [[Saell-Terae]], a [[Yarkora/Legends|Yarkora]] antiquities dealer, later upgraded. Like its exterior, the interior setting of the ''Lost Reef'' was similar to other ''DeepWater''-class ships, consisting of a [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] in front, two cargo bays on the sides, crew lounge and state rooms in the middle, and an engineering compartment at the rear. The ship was stocked with two [[Standard month/Legends|months]]' worth of consumables. The ''Lost Reef'' carried three different landing systems: the standard landing gear and the [[landing claw]]—which was used when docking with [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] or other ships, with the extendable boarding tube connected to the [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]]—were accompanied by puncture-proof pontoons designed for [[Ocean/Legends|sea]] level landings. One of these pontoons held a [[Smuggling compartment/Legends|hidden compartment]] which Saell-Terae used to hide [[Three warrior statues|three statues]] that he acquired in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Saell-Terae made multiple modifications to the ship while it was in his possession. He replaced the [[Life support/Legends|life support]] system, which had been designed for Mon Calamari, with one more suitable for use by [[Amphibian/Legends|non-amphibious]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. He upgraded the engines as well, which increased the ship's maneuverability and speed, giving it the maximum speed of 1,050 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] conditions. The [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was also upgraded from standard x2 to x1, and the backup system from x15 to x10. The basic weapon systems found in ''DeepWater''-class ships were also replaced and improved, with the ''Lost Reef'' being equipped with a [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannon]] turret, instead of the usual dual [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser turret]], and four hidden [[Missile launcher/Legends|torpedo launchers]], designed to be used underwater. These weapons—along with military-grade shielding and an improved [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]] that Saell-Terae installed—were illegal on civilian ships according to Imperial restrictions. The later owner of the ship, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Jorj Car'das]]—who had received the ship from his friend [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who was a [[Captain of the Line/Legends|senior captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], at some point before [[0 ABY/Legends|0.8 ABY]]—added further modifications to the craft. ==History== {{Quote|Strictly speaking, a tractor beam doesn't pull in its target, but instead pulls the target and the generator toward their common center of mass. Since the ''Chimaera'' vastly outmasses nearly everything you've ever drawn in, it's never before been an issue for you.''"
"''Yes, sir.''"
"''The question now, of course, is whether the ''Lost Reef'' and I can provide you enough of a nudge to pull you off Nuso Esva's predetermined path.|Thrawn, explaining his plan to save ''Chimaera'' to Commander Pellaeon|}} Mon Calamari Shipyards manufactured the ''Lost Reef'' along with around 10,000 other ''DeepWater''-class light freighters for the Mon Calamari Commercial Expeditionary Service. When the Galactic Empire dissolved the Commercial Expeditionary Service, the ''Lost Reef'' and its sister ships were put to auction and sold to individual owners. The ''Lost Reef'' was purchased for 76,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] by a Yarkora named Saell-Terae who, like many others of his species, claimed to be an antiques dealer. Saell-Terae used his ship to explore the Unknown Regions; his negotiations at the edges of space, however, did not appear to be profitable. [[File:Thrawn_TFUCG.jpg|thumb|right|300px|After obtaining the ''Lost Reef'', Thrawn (pictured) gave the ship to his friend, Car'das.]] During his travels in the Unknown Regions, Saell-Terae acquired three warrior statues, made by a [[Unidentified military commander|military commander]] who—despite his [[Unidentified species (Unknown Regions)|species]]' primitive spacefaring culture—was at war with Thrawn, a [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] who had been sent to work in the Unknown Regions. Having found out that Saell-Terae possessed these wooden statues, covered with precious gems and valuable metals, Thrawn sent his [[Agent/Legends|agents]] to find the Yarkora and retrieve them, as their design could help the Chiss learn more about his opponent. Pursued by Thrawn's agents, Saell-Terae was forced to abandon his ship near the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]] after deleting his [[Navigation computer/Legends|navcomputer]] records. When the ship was discovered drifting, however, a [[Dataplaque/Legends|dataplaque]]—which held detailed [[Astrogation map|charts]] of parts of the Unknown Regions—was hidden in a secret compartment located in the ''Lost Reef''{{'s}} airlock. The three warrior statues were also still concealed in the pontoon, carefully protected with [[cargo netting]] and [[packing foam]]. When the ''Lost Reef'' was discovered drifting, Saell-Terae wanted to reclaim his ship, and he was even prepared to [[Death/Legends|kill]] whoever possessed it. At the same time, Thrawn's agents were also still seeking to deliver the ''Lost Reef'' and the warrior statues to their master. Eventually the ship fell into the hands of Thrawn, who gave it to his Human friend Jorj Car'das, the head of a large smuggling and [[Information broker/Legends|information brokering]] [[Car'das/Legends|organization]]. About eight months after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], when Car'das's health had started to deteriorate, Thrawn requested his help in stopping [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Nuso Esva]]. Together Car'das and Thrawn traveled to the [[Forest/Legends|Forest]] [[Moon/Legends|Moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], where Thrawn arranged a meeting with [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] on board the ''Lost Reef''. Thrawn gave the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] his suggestions on improving the defenses set around the construction site of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], a new moon-sized [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] that the Empire was building. He also made a request for six more [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to help him address the threat that Esva's [[Nuso Esva's battle fleet|fleet]] posed, but the Emperor turned him down, advising the Chiss to speak with his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], instead. After meeting with Vader, Car'das and Thrawn used the ''Lost Reef'' to follow the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], which Esva had infiltrated under a false identity, to the Unknown Regions. Soon an emergency call from [[Poln Major]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Candoras sector/Legends|Candoras sector]], reached them and ordered all Imperial vessels to defend the [[Star system/Legends|system]] against a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] threat. In the [[Poln system/Legends|Poln system]] they found that ''Chimaera'', which Esva had crippled, was on a collision course with the [[Golan I Space Defense Platform/Legends|Golan station]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] Poln Major. Car'das and Thrawn used the ''Lost Reef'' and ''Chimaera''{{'s}} remaining [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] systems to steer the seriously damaged ship away from the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] and ruined Esva's [[Battle of the Poln system|battle plan]], which had rested on Thrawn being forced to sacrifice either the warship or the Golan station. When Esva had brought in the rest of his [[Eastern Fleet|troops]], Thrawn also invited his reinforcements to the scene: [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader and his private task force known as the [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], a group consisting of the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] and six Star Destroyers: the [[Emancipator|''Accuser'']], the [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Adjudicator'']], the [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']], the [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], the ''[[Stalker/Legends|Stalker]]'', and the [[Rebel Dream|''Tyrant'']]. As Thrawn was still on board the ''Lost Reef'', he transferred command of [[Task Force Admonitor|his task force]] to Vader for the rest of the battle. One third of Esva's entire [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was lost in battle, and after conversing with Vader, Thrawn was eager to head back out to the Unknown Regions and use the advantage he had gained. Before leaving, Thrawn suggested that Car'das should travel to the planet [[Wroona/Legends|Wroona]] and rescue the people whom Esva was holding hostage there. Esva was using the people on Wroona to blackmail a Human named [[Sorro]], forcing him to act as his [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Despite being seriously ill and knowing his only hope for a cure laid in the outlying [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], Car'das made plans to visit Wroona and see if he and the ''Lost Reef'' could be of help. ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|My hope is that Sorro's family may still be alive and can be freed.''"
"''By me.''"
"''There's no one else who can do the job. The ''Lost Reef'' has the weaponry, and I'm sure you have the necessary contacts to locate them. The question is whether you have the will.|Thrawn, persuading Car'das to help the distressed people of Wroona|}} [[File:Jorj.jpg|thumb|left|140px|Jorj Car'das acquired the ''Lost Reef'' from Thrawn.]] The first individual owner of the ''Lost Reef'' was a Yarkora called Saell-Terae. Before he bought the ship from an auction, it was owned by Mon Calamari Commercial Expeditionary Service, a company that the Galactic Empire closed down. Like other Yarkora—a species encountered only in small numbers, the members of which often claimed to have specialized in antiques and little-known religions—Saell-Terae kept his ultimate mission quiet, using the ''Lost Reef'' to travel the little-studied [[Hyperspace route/Legends|starlanes]] of the Unknown Regions with minimal gain for himself. After Saell-Terae lost his ship, it fell into the hands of Senior [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Thrawn, who gave it to his friend Jorj Car'das. The smuggler leader was in possession of the ''Lost Reef'' long enough to make multiple modifications to the ship, before he joined Thrawn's effort to crush Nuso Esva. When saying goodbyes to Thrawn after the battle of the Poln system, Car'das planned to first travel to Wroona and attempt to rescue Esva's hostages, before he would continue toward the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], where he hoped he could improve his failing health. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Lost Reef'' was first described in the [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Stock Ships]]'', where it appeared as an example ship for the ''DeepWater''-class light freighter. The ship later appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s novel ''[[Choices of One]]'', where it is used by Jorj Car'das. These two sources create a minor inconsistency concerning Thrawn's rank in the Imperial military: ''Stock Ships'' grants him the title of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], while he is only a senior captain during the events of ''Choices of One''. This article assumes that the events of ''Stock Ships'' take place before those described in ''Choices of One''. ==Appearances== *''[[Choices of One]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Stock Ships]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Verlorenes Riff |fi=Lost Reef }} [[Category:DeepWater-class light freighters]] [[Category:Smuggler starships]]
